name: Kurdus ibn Zuhayr
title: Devoted Brother
shortDescription: One of the old, loyal companions of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) in Kufa who joined Imam Hussain (AS) at night with his brothers.
fullStory:
Kurdus ibn Zuhayr al-Taghlibi, along with his brothers (Qasit and Muqsit), were sincere Shias and veteran companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) in Kufa. They had fought alongside Imam Ali (AS) in the battles of Jamal, Siffin, and Nahrawan.

When Ibn Ziyad's army besieged Kufa and conditions became difficult for Shias, these three old and experienced brothers bravely and cleverly snuck out of Kufa at night.

They reached Imam Hussain's (AS) camp on the night of Ashura (or before). Kurdus, who had spent his life defending Wilayah, could not leave the Imam of his time alone.

On Ashura, he and his brothers fought bravely during the first attack (the morning shower of arrows), and all three attained martyrdom together. Their names are immortalized in the Ziyarat al-Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى قاسِطٍ وَكُرْدُوسٍ ابْنَيْ زُهَيْرٍ التَّغْلِبِيَّيْنِ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Qasit and Kurdus, the sons of Zuhayr al-Taghlibi...
